title: Add user C/C++ code to final achieve in default lib mode (#4749)
body:
Close #4749.

The first commit is an independent small cleanup. We should use `$(VK_OBJS) $(VM_HIER_LIBS)` directly.

The second commit is the main work. 

Basically it changes make rules in default lib mode as follows:
```
default: lib{prefix}
lib{prefix}.a: $(VK_USER_OBJS) $(VK_OBJS) $(VM_HIER)
libverilated_rt.a: $(VK_GLOBAL_OBJS)
lib{prefix}: lib{prefix}.a libverilated_rt.a
```

For users who write their own Makefile, they shall not be affected. I don't do any backward incompatible change here.
For users who invokes Makefile directly, it will generate `lib{prefix}.a` and `libverilated_rt.a`, which can be used directly in final linking process. It should be an improvement.

I intend to add a simple test, but I have difficulties in this step. I'm not familiar with Perl.
Wilson, may you give me some instructions? e.g. which test case I could take as a reference.
